_readme: AUTO-GENERATED from .announcements during `bun run release:version`. Each item is one released version's in-app announcement. Do not edit released entries by hand.

items.text: Coding agents in Helmor can now drive Helmor itself — spawn sibling workspaces, dispatch ship actions, and read other agents' transcripts. Just ask them in any chat. Helmor CLI and skills install automatically in the background.
Every new workspace now has a gitignored `.agent-contexts/` directory agents can use to share files across sessions without polluting diffs.
Slack, GitHub, and GitLab context detail panels now refresh automatically when you open them or refocus Helmor, and there's a manual refresh button in the toolbar.
Cmd+Shift+N now opens the new-workspace composer in Just-chat mode, and Cmd+N opens it in worktree mode.
Slack is now a Context source — connect your workspaces and the agent can read threads (with images) the same way you do.
Local LLM lands as a very experimental preview — it powers session title and branch name generation for now. More features coming soon.
Desktop notifications can now play a sound — pick from 12 effects in Settings → General.

Each run script can now declare an optional cleanup command that runs when you click Stop — handy for docker compose, supabase, or anything with external resources to tear down. A second Stop click force-kills if you don't want to wait.
You can now configure multiple run scripts per repository
Press ArrowUp / ArrowDown in the composer to step through previously-sent prompts for the current session — like bash or zsh.
Four new color themes are now available — Aubergine, Hoth, Choco Mint, and Banana.
Workspaces can now archive themselves automatically once their linked PR/MR is merged.
You can now edit code right from the diff view.
Send Helmor feedback right from the app — file a GitHub issue or hit "Quick fix" to open a workspace with the feedback as the first prompt.
Pick "Just Chat" on the start page to open a workspace without binding it to a repo.
You can now reuse an existing branch when creating a workspace.
You can now hold Ctrl+Tab to cycle through recently used workspaces — release to switch, just like Cmd+Tab.
The sidebar now has view controls for filtering by repository, changing grouping, and choosing sort order.
The Git header now shows when PR checks are still running or GitHub is blocking a merge.
You can now choose a separate font for embedded terminals.
You can now turn inspector terminal hover expansion on or off in Settings.
Codex /goal continuations no longer ask for approval.
Appearance settings now let you customize chat size, UI and code fonts, and pointer cursors.
Codex slash commands now include skills from linked directories and the repository root.
Claude sessions now include the /goal command for setting a completion condition that Claude keeps working toward.
You can now drag workspaces in the sidebar to keep each section in your preferred order.
You can now group workspaces in the sidebar by repository — toggle it in Settings.
Add Context now supports GitLab too — auto-detected based on your current project.
Claude Code thinking now offers two modes. Choosing Omitted lets the final text stream sooner.
Plus a batch of performance fixes across the app.
